items.title: 'Non-event': World Cup hotel bookings lagging far behind expectations, survey shows
items.summary: US President Donald Trump has frequently touted the country's host status for world football's centrepiece event, but he has also launched a wide-ranging crackdown on visas for visitors to the United States.

items.title: Three child protection workers stood down after Kumanjayi Little Baby's death
items.summary: The alleged murder of Kumanjayi Little Baby has led to three workers being stood down.

items.title: Albanese unveils $10bn fuel plan with billion-litre government stockpile
items.summary: Government-owned diesel and aviation fuel reserves will be established as part of a broader reform package responding to the fuel crisis.

items.title: Some IS-linked women set to soon arrive in Australia will be arrested, police say
items.summary: The group, which the federal government insists was not assisted in returning to Australia, is expected to arrive on Thursday night.

items.title: Tax breaks, super changes to kick in on 1 July as Chalmers flags more to come in budget
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has pointed to a number of other policies kicking in, amid muted cost of living budget expectations.

items.title: Bondi accused charged with 19 new offences while in custody
items.summary: The new charges include ten counts of shooting with intent to murder during Australia's deadliest mass shooting in almost three decades.

items.title: Trump pauses day-old Hormuz operation in latest push for Iran deal
items.summary: The so-called "Project Freedom" was supposed to help vessels depart the Strait of Hormuz under US guard.

items.title: Sina was forced to weigh a 'dreaded' decision — and fears the rate hike will spark another
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia has warned that the economic outlook is not good for Australians.

items.title: Experts warn Australians are underestimating flu risk as winter looms
items.summary: More than 1,700 deaths were recorded last flu season, but experts say Australians still underestimate the risks.

items.title: Three people evacuated from hantavirus-hit ship as contact tracing efforts sharpen
items.summary: Spain is allowing a cruise ship hit by hantavirus and three deaths to dock in the Canary Islands, as authorities investigate if transmission occurred on board.

items.title: 'A really difficult time': RBA hikes rates for the third time this year — as it happened
items.summary: The Reserve Bank was widely expected to hike interest rates at its board meeting today.

items.title: Award-winning author Craig Silvey pleads guilty to child exploitation crimes
items.summary: Silvey was at one point one of Australia's most celebrated authors, behind popular books such as Jasper Jones and Rhubarb.

items.title: Heavy metal diplomacy: Australia and Japan strike deals on minerals, energy and defence
items.summary: Japan and Australia have signed a sweeping package of agreements — sealed with an AC/DC drum skin and a very expensive melon.

items.title: Australians on board virus-hit cruise ship as passenger makes emotional plea
items.summary: A US travel blogger onboard the stranded ship said there was "a lot of uncertainty" about what would happen next.

items.title: The RBA's rates call could 'come down to the wire'. This is what it means for you
items.summary: If the RBA decides to increase the cash rate, it will be the third hike this year.

items.title: Albanese strikes deal with Japan on defence, minerals and fuel security

items.title: Leagh accidentally saw a photo on her partner's phone. It put her in an impossible position
items.summary: These Australians found themselves caught in moral dilemmas. Here's what they decided.

items.title: 'Schools that look like prisons': Jewish Australians share stories in antisemitism inquiry
items.summary: The mother of a young girl who witnessed the Bondi attack last year told the inquiry Jewish children are growing up in fear.

items.title: What is hantavirus, the suspected disease behind a deadly cruise ship outbreak?
items.summary: Here's what we know about the suspected outbreak of a rare infection that killed three people on a cruise ship on the Atlantic Ocean.

items.title: Trump says US will start a project to 'guide' stranded ships out of Hormuz from Monday
items.summary: It comes a day after Trump said he'd likely reject the latest Iranian proposal because "they have not paid a big enough price".

items.title: It's probably already in your fridge — and it could help in cutting risk of Alzheimer's
items.summary: Eating seafood twice a week can be important to protect the brain from different diseases.

items.title: The mental health 'safety gap' causing decade-long delays for vulnerable Australians
items.summary: From referral to consultation, accessing mental health care presents many challenges for some Australians, a new report has found.

items.title: Teen radicalisation fears drive major online counter‑terror overhaul
items.summary: Spies will monitor gaming platforms as authorities warn extremists are targeting young people through gaming platforms and chat groups.

items.title: Antisemitism hearing told man's public assault largely ignored on busy street
